data.content:
### Run Auth-js Integration Tests - Bash

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

Execute integration tests for auth-js package which requires Docker infrastructure including GoTrue and PostgreSQL. Includes separate commands for test suite, infrastructure setup, and cleanup.

```bash
nx test:auth auth-js
nx test:suite auth-js
nx test:infra auth-js
nx test:clean auth-js
```

--------------------------------

### Check code formatting with Nx

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

Verify if the code in the Nx workspace adheres to the predefined formatting rules without making any changes. This command is useful for CI/CD pipelines or local checks to ensure compliance before committing or submitting a pull request.

```bash
nx format:check
```

--------------------------------

### Manual Infrastructure Management for Auth-js Tests

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/CLAUDE.md

Provides individual commands for managing Docker infrastructure and running tests separately during development. This allows developers to run tests multiple times without recreating Docker containers each time.

```bash
# Auth-js
nx test:infra auth-js   # Start Docker containers
nx test:suite auth-js   # Run tests (can run multiple times)
nx test:clean auth-js   # Cleanup
```

--------------------------------

### GitHub Actions OIDC Permissions Configuration for JSR

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/docs/JSR_PUBLISHING.md

YAML configuration snippet showing the required permissions for JSR publishing in GitHub Actions workflows. This enables automatic OIDC authentication without managing secrets or tokens manually, providing short-lived token security.

```yaml
permissions:
  contents: read
  id-token: write # Required for JSR OIDC authentication
```

--------------------------------

### Run Storage-js Integration Tests - Bash

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

Execute integration tests for storage-js package requiring Docker infrastructure with Storage API, PostgreSQL, and Kong. Provides commands for running test suite, infrastructure, and cleanup.

```bash
nx test:storage storage-js
nx test:suite storage-js
nx test:infra storage-js
nx test:clean storage-js
```

--------------------------------

### Run Functions-js Integration Tests - Bash

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

Execute integration tests for functions-js package which uses Deno relay via testcontainers Docker infrastructure. Includes CI-specific command with coverage reporting.

```bash
nx test functions-js
nx test:ci functions-js
```

--------------------------------

### Run linting on affected libraries using Nx

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

Execute linting checks specifically on the libraries within the Nx workspace that have been modified or are dependent on modified code. This helps to quickly identify and fix code quality or style issues related to recent changes.

```bash
nx affected --target=lint
```

--------------------------------

### Format Code - Bash

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

Format all code in the workspace using configured formatting rules, or check formatting without applying fixes.

```bash
nx format
nx format:check
```

--------------------------------

### Format code using Nx

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

Automatically format the code in the Nx workspace according to predefined styling rules. This command helps maintain consistent code style across the project and is often used as part of pre-commit or pre-PR checks.

```bash
nx format
```

--------------------------------

### Manual Infrastructure Management for Storage-js Tests

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/CLAUDE.md

Provides individual commands for managing Docker infrastructure and running tests separately during development. This allows developers to run tests multiple times without recreating Docker containers each time.

```bash
# Storage-js
nx test:infra storage-js   # Start Docker containers
nx test:suite storage-js   # Run tests (can run multiple times)
nx test:clean storage-js   # Cleanup
```

--------------------------------

### Run Nx Commands for Build, Test, and Format

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

This snippet provides essential Nx commands for running affected tests, builds, and formatting code within the monorepository. These commands are crucial for maintaining code quality and ensuring project health before any submission.

```bash
nx affected --target=test
nx affected --target=build
nx format
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Dependency Graph - Bash

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

Generate and display an interactive dependency graph of the Supabase monorepo workspace, showing relationships between all packages.

```bash
nx graph
```

--------------------------------

### Troubleshoot Test Failures After Pulling Changes

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

These commands address test failures that may occur after pulling the latest changes from the repository. The process ensures dependencies are up to date, affected packages are rebuilt, and tests are re-executed to confirm stability.

```bash
npm install

nx affected --target=build

nx affected --target=test
```

--------------------------------

### PUT /auth/v1/user - Update User Metadata

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/supabase-js/llms.txt

Updates the authenticated user's profile information or custom metadata.

```APIDOC
## PUT /auth/v1/user

### Description
Updates the authenticated user's profile information or custom metadata.

### Method
PUT

### Endpoint
/auth/v1/user

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **data** (object) - Optional - An object containing fields to update within the user's `user_metadata`.
    - **full_name** (string) - Optional - The user's updated full name.
    - *...other_custom_fields* (any) - Optional - Any other custom metadata fields to update.
- **email** (string) - Optional - The user's new email address. Requires email confirmation.
- **password** (string) - Optional - The user's new password.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "data": {
    "full_name": "Jane Doe",
    "age": 26
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **user** (object) - The updated user object, reflecting the changes.
    - **id** (string) - The unique identifier for the user.
    - **email** (string) - The user's email address.
    - **user_metadata** (object) - The updated custom metadata associated with the user.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "user": {
    "id": "some-uuid-1",
    "email": "user@example.com",
    "user_metadata": {
      "full_name": "Jane Doe",
      "age": 26
    }
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Lint Code - Bash

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

Run linting checks across all packages or specific packages in the Supabase monorepo. Supports linting entire workspace or individual libraries.

```bash
nx run-many --target=lint --all
nx lint auth-js
```

--------------------------------

### Run PostgREST-js Integration Tests - Bash

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

Execute integration tests for postgrest-js package which requires Docker with PostgREST and PostgreSQL. Includes individual commands for running, updating, and type checking tests.

```bash
nx test:ci:postgrest postgrest-js
nx test:run postgrest-js
nx test:update postgrest-js
nx test:types postgrest-js
```

--------------------------------

### Run Supabase JavaScript SDK Tests with Nx

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

Commands for running comprehensive test suites across different Supabase JavaScript SDKs. These commands handle specific test setups like Docker for `auth-js` and `storage-js`, testcontainers for `functions-js`, or no Docker for `realtime-js`, and unit tests for `supabase-js`.

```bash
# Run complete test suites (handles Docker setup/cleanup automatically)
nx test:auth auth-js                    # Complete auth-js test suite
nx test:storage storage-js              # Complete storage-js test suite
nx test:ci:postgrest postgrest-js      # Complete postgrest-js test suite
nx test functions-js                    # Standard test (uses testcontainers)
nx test realtime-js                     # Standard test (no Docker needed)
nx test supabase-js                     # Standard test (unit tests only)
```

--------------------------------

### Run complete test suites for Supabase-JS libraries using Nx

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

Execute comprehensive test suites for individual Supabase-JS client libraries, including those requiring Docker-based infrastructure (auth-js, storage-js, postgrest-js) and those without (functions-js, realtime-js, supabase-js). These commands automatically handle Docker setup where needed.

```bash
# Packages with Docker requirements
nx test:auth auth-js
nx test:storage storage-js
nx test:ci:postgrest postgrest-js

# Packages without Docker requirements
nx test functions-js
nx test realtime-js
nx test supabase-js
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Postgrest-JS Services with NX

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/CLAUDE.md

These `nx` commands are used to manage the `postgrest-js` library's development lifecycle within the monorepo. They facilitate starting Docker containers for the service, executing its test suite, and cleaning up associated resources.

```bash
nx db:run postgrest-js      # Start containers
nx test:run postgrest-js    # Run tests
nx db:clean postgrest-js    # Cleanup
```

--------------------------------

### Build affected libraries using Nx

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

Identify and build only the libraries in the Nx workspace that have been affected by recent code changes, along with their dependencies. This command is efficient for incremental builds in a large monorepo, speeding up development cycles.

```bash
nx affected --target=build
```

--------------------------------

### Show Affected Projects - Bash

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

Identify and display all projects affected by current changes in the workspace with an interactive graph visualization.

```bash
nx affected --graph
```

--------------------------------

### Troubleshoot Build Failures by Resetting Nx and npm

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

This sequence of commands provides a comprehensive solution for unexpected build failures. It involves clearing the Nx cache, removing and reinstalling node modules, and then rebuilding the entire project from scratch.

```bash
nx reset

rm -rf node_modules
npm install

nx run-many --target=build --all
```

--------------------------------

### Troubleshooting Cache and Installation Issues - Bash

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/CLAUDE.md

Commands for resolving common development issues including clearing Nx cache, rebuilding all projects, performing clean installation, checking for linting errors, and running specific test files. Useful for resolving mysterious build failures or stale cache problems.

```bash
# Clear cache and rebuild
nx reset
nx run-many --target=build --all

# Clean install
rm -rf node_modules
npm install

# Check for errors
nx run-many --target=lint --all

# Run specific test file (for packages with standard test target)
nx test realtime-js --testFile=RealtimeClient.test.ts
nx test supabase-js --testFile=client.test.ts
```

--------------------------------

### Quick Fix Workflow with Git and Nx

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/CLAUDE.md

Demonstrates the workflow for committing quick fixes to single libraries with automatic canary versioning and test execution. The process includes committing changes with conventional commits, running package-specific tests, and manual promotion to stable releases.

```bash
# Even for single-library fixes:
git add .
git commit -m "fix(auth-js): correct session expiry calculation"
# → Automatic canary: 2.80.1-canary.0 published to 'canary' dist-tag
# Run complete test suite for the package:
nx test:auth auth-js
# After validation, manual promotion:
nx release --tag=latest --yes  # Promotes to stable with same version for ALL packages
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Hybrid Nx Releases with Bash Commands

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/CLAUDE.md

This collection of `nx release` commands facilitates both automated canary releases and manual stable promotions. It includes commands for previewing changes, forcing specific version bumps (patch, minor, major), promoting specific canary versions, and publishing to `canary` or `latest` dist-tags.

```bash
# Canary releases (automated in CI)
nx release --tag=canary --yes
nx release --specifier=prerelease --tag=canary

# Stable releases (manual promotion)
nx release --tag=latest --yes
nx release --specifier=2.80.1 --tag=latest --yes

# Preview and debugging
nx release --dry-run
nx release --dry-run --verbose
nx release --first-release --dry-run

# Version-specific stable releases
nx release --specifier=patch --tag=latest
nx release --specifier=minor --tag=latest
nx release --specifier=major --tag=latest
```

--------------------------------

### Complete Test Suites with Docker - Auth-js

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/CLAUDE.md

Runs the complete auth-js test suite with automatic Docker setup and cleanup. This is the recommended approach for comprehensive testing as it handles infrastructure management automatically.

```bash
# Auth-js: Complete test suite (handles Docker setup/cleanup automatically)
nx test:auth auth-js
```

--------------------------------

### Pre-Pull Request Checks and Validation - Bash

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/CLAUDE.md

Provides a comprehensive checklist of commands to run before creating a pull request including branch updates, code formatting, testing affected packages, linting, and building. Ensures all CI/CD checks will pass and code quality standards are met.

```bash
git checkout master
git pull upstream master
git checkout your-feature-branch
git rebase master

nx format                    # Format code
# Run complete test suite for affected packages individually
nx test:auth auth-js         # If auth-js changed
nx test:storage storage-js   # If storage-js changed
# ... etc for other packages
nx affected --target=lint    # Lint affected packages
nx affected --target=build   # Build affected packages
```

--------------------------------

### Show Workspace Projects - Bash

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

Display all projects available in the Supabase monorepo workspace, providing visibility into all libraries and packages.

```bash
nx show projects
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Package Exports for Multiple Environments

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/packages/core/storage-js/README.md

Defines package.json export configuration that provides appropriate module formats for different environments: CommonJS for Node.js, ES Modules for modern bundlers, TypeScript definitions, and UMD for CDN usage. Each export field points to the correct build artifact for its target environment.

```json
{
  "main": "dist/main/index.js",
  "module": "dist/module/index.js",
  "types": "dist/module/index.d.ts",
  "jsdelivr": "dist/umd/supabase.js",
  "unpkg": "dist/umd/supabase.js"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Complete Test Suites with Docker - Postgrest-js

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/CLAUDE.md

Runs the complete postgrest-js test suite with automatic Docker setup and cleanup using the CI-specific test command. This is the recommended approach for comprehensive testing.

```bash
# Postgrest-js: Complete test suite (handles Docker setup/cleanup automatically)
nx test:ci:postgrest postgrest-js
```

--------------------------------

### Use Interactive Commit Tool - Bash

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/CLAUDE.md

Guides developers to use the npm-based interactive commit tool instead of git commit directly. This ensures all commits follow conventional commit format with proper scope, type, and message structure as required by the repository validation.

```bash
npm run commit  # Guides you through proper format
```

--------------------------------

### POST /auth/v1/token - Sign In with Email and Password

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/supabase-js/llms.txt

Authenticates an existing user using their email and password, returning a new session if successful.

```APIDOC
## POST /auth/v1/token

### Description
Authenticates an existing user using their email and password, returning a new session if successful.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/auth/v1/token

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **email** (string) - Required - The user's email address.
- **password** (string) - Required - The user's password.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "password": "secure-password-123"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **user** (object) - The authenticated user object.
    - **id** (string) - The unique identifier for the user.
    - **email** (string) - The user's email address.
- **session** (object) - The user's session object.
    - **access_token** (string) - The access token for the session.
    - **expires_at** (number) - Unix timestamp indicating when the session expires.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "user": {
    "id": "some-uuid-1",
    "email": "user@example.com"
  },
  "session": {
    "access_token": "eyJ...",
    "expires_at": 1678886400
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Clean Up Docker Resources

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

This command prunes the Docker system, removing unused containers, networks, images, and volumes. It helps free up disk space and can resolve various Docker-related issues, ensuring a clean testing environment.

```bash
docker system prune
```

--------------------------------

### Nx Graph and Dependency Analysis Commands - Bash

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/CLAUDE.md

Collection of Nx commands for analyzing project structure, dependencies, and affected packages. These commands help visualize the monorepo layout, understand project relationships, and identify which packages are affected by changes.

```bash
# Visualize dependency graph
nx graph

# Show what's affected by changes
nx affected --graph

# List all projects
nx show projects

# See project details
nx show project auth-js --web

# Clear Nx cache
nx reset
```

--------------------------------

### Build Supabase Auth JS Package in Monorepo using Nx

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/packages/core/auth-js/README.md

Provides commands for building the `auth-js` package when working within the Supabase JavaScript monorepo. It includes options for a complete build, watch mode for development, and specific build targets for CommonJS and ES Modules outputs, along with linting and type checking.

```bash
# Complete build (from monorepo root)
npx nx build auth-js

# Build with watch mode for development
npx nx build auth-js --watch

# Individual build targets
npx nx build:main auth-js    # CommonJS build (dist/main/)
npx nx build:module auth-js  # ES Modules build (dist/module/)

# Other useful commands
npx nx lint auth-js          # Run ESLint
npx nx typecheck auth-js     # TypeScript type checking
npx nx docs auth-js          # Generate documentation
```

--------------------------------

### Generate test coverage reports for Supabase-JS libraries with Nx

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

Run tests and simultaneously generate code coverage reports for specific Supabase-JS libraries. This helps in assessing the extent to which the source code is executed during testing. Examples include supabase-js, realtime-js, and functions-js.

```bash
# Test with coverage
nx test supabase-js --coverage
nx test:coverage realtime-js
nx test:ci functions-js
```

--------------------------------

### Complete Test Suites with Docker - Storage-js

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/CLAUDE.md

Runs the complete storage-js test suite with automatic Docker setup and cleanup. This is the recommended approach for comprehensive testing as it handles infrastructure management automatically.

```bash
# Storage-js: Complete test suite (handles Docker setup/cleanup automatically)
nx test:storage storage-js
```

--------------------------------

### Query Similar Vectors in Supabase Vector Index (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/packages/core/storage-js/README.md

This example illustrates performing an Approximate Nearest Neighbor (ANN) search to find vectors similar to a given query vector. It allows specifying the number of top results (`topK`), applying metadata filters, and requesting the distance and metadata of the matches.

```typescript
const { data, error } = await index.queryVectors({
  queryVector: {
    float32: [
      /* 1536 numbers */
    ],
  },
  topK: 10,
  filter: {
    category: 'technical',
    published: true,
  },
  returnDistance: true,
  returnMetadata: true,
})

// Results ordered by similarity
data?.matches.forEach((match) => {
  console.log(`${match.key}: distance=${match.distance}`)
})
```

--------------------------------

### Test Specific Supabase JS Packages with Docker

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/CLAUDE.md

These commands execute complete test suites for various Supabase JS packages, handling Docker setup and cleanup where required. Tests ensure functionality across different parts of the monorepo, including `auth-js`, `storage-js`, `postgrest-js`, `functions-js`, `realtime-js`, and `supabase-js`.

```bash
# Test specific packages (complete test suites with Docker setup/cleanup)
nx test:auth auth-js                    # Complete auth-js test suite (handles Docker)
nx test:storage storage-js              # Complete storage-js test suite (handles Docker)
nx test:ci:postgrest postgrest-js      # Complete postgrest-js test suite (handles Docker)
nx test functions-js                    # Standard test (uses testcontainers)
nx test realtime-js                     # Standard test (no Docker needed)
nx test supabase-js                     # Standard test (unit tests only)
```

--------------------------------

### Update User Metadata in JavaScript

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/supabase-js/llms.txt

Modifies custom user metadata fields for the authenticated user. Accepts an object with metadata properties to update. Returns updated user data on success.

```javascript
const { data: updateData, error: updateError } = await auth.updateUser({
  data: { full_name: 'Jane Doe' }
})
```

--------------------------------

### Clear Nx workspace cache

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

Reset the Nx computational cache, which stores results of previous builds, tests, and other tasks. This ensures that subsequent commands execute without relying on potentially stale cached data, useful for troubleshooting or forced rebuilds.

```bash
nx reset
```

--------------------------------

### Standard Library Directory Structure

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

This snippet illustrates the conventional directory structure adopted by each library within the `packages/core` directory. It defines the standard locations for source code, tests, documentation, infrastructure, and configuration files, promoting consistency across the project.

```text
packages/core/[library]/
├── src/              # Source code
├── test/             # Test files (mirrors src/ structure)
├── docs/             # API documentation
├── infra/            # Docker infrastructure (if needed)
├── package.json      # Library-specific dependencies
├── tsconfig.json     # TypeScript configuration
├── jest.config.js    # Jest configuration
└── README.md         # Library documentation
```

--------------------------------

### Install Supabase.js Preview Release Version from PR

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

This command installs a preview version of the `@supabase/supabase-js` package directly from a Pull Request. Preview versions are generated via `pkg.pr.new` when a PR has a `trigger: preview` label, allowing contributors to test changes before merging.

```bash
npm install https://pkg.pr.new/@supabase/supabase-js@[pr-number]
```

--------------------------------

### Insert Vectors with Metadata into Supabase Index

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/supabase-js/llms.txt

Illustrates adding multiple vectors along with their associated metadata into an existing vector index. Each vector requires a unique key, data (e.g., float32 array), and optional metadata for filtering and retrieval.

```javascript
const index = bucket.index('documents-openai')
await index.putVectors({
  vectors: [
    {
      key: 'doc-1',
      data: { float32: new Array(1536).fill(0).map(() => Math.random()) },
      metadata: { title: 'Introduction', category: 'docs', page: 1 }
    },
    {
      key: 'doc-2',
      data: { float32: new Array(1536).fill(0).map(() => Math.random()) },
      metadata: { title: 'Getting Started', category: 'docs', page: 2 }
    }
  ]
})
```

--------------------------------

### Run Realtime-js Unit Tests - Bash

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

Execute tests for realtime-js package which uses mock WebSockets and does not require Docker infrastructure. Includes coverage and watch mode options.

```bash
nx test realtime-js
nx test:coverage realtime-js
nx test:watch realtime-js
```

--------------------------------

### Run Supabase Auth JS Tests in Monorepo using Nx and Docker

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/packages/core/auth-js/README.md

Outlines the commands for running the comprehensive test suite for the `auth-js` package. This process requires Docker to be installed and running, as it spins up a local Supabase Auth server (GoTrue) and PostgreSQL database for testing purposes.

```bash
# Run complete test suite (from monorepo root)
npx nx test:auth auth-js

# Run just the test suite (requires infrastructure to be running)
npx nx test:suite auth-js

# Manually manage test infrastructure
npx nx test:infra auth-js   # Start Docker containers
npx nx test:clean auth-js   # Stop and remove containers
```

--------------------------------

### Conventional Commit Message Format Template

Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js/blob/master/WARP.md

This template outlines the required structure for Conventional Commit messages, comprising a type, optional scope, description, and optional body and footers. Adhering to this format is crucial for automated release processes and consistent history.

```text
<type>(<scope>): <description>

[optional body]

[optional footer(s)]
```
